Name: Tim Sort
Author: Tim Peters
Description: Timsort is a hybrid stable sorting algorithm, derived from merge sort and insertion sort, designed to perform well on many kinds of real-world data. It was implemented by Tim Peters in 2002 for use in the Python programming language. The algorithm finds subsequences of the data that are already ordered (runs) and uses them to sort the remainder more efficiently.
Algorithm: async function sort(){const r=async(r,a)=>{for(let t=r+1;t<=a;t++){const a=array[t];let e=t-1;for(;e>=r;){await compare(e,array.indexOf(a));if(array[e]>a){if(firstRun)return;swap(e,e+1),await sleep(),e--}else break}if(firstRun)return;swap(e+1,array.indexOf(a)),await sleep()}},a=async(r,a,t)=>{if(a>=t)return;const e=a-r+1,n=t-a,f=Array(e),i=Array(n);for(let a=0;a<e;a++)f[a]=array[r+a];for(let r=0;r<n;r++)i[r]=array[a+1+r];let s=0,l=0,o=r;for(;s<f.length&&l<i.length;){await compare(array.indexOf(f[s]),array.indexOf(i[l]));if(f[s]<=i[l]){if(firstRun)return;swap(o++,array.indexOf(f[s++])),await sleep()}else{if(firstRun)return;swap(o++,array.indexOf(i[l++])),await sleep()}}for(;s<f.length;){if(firstRun)return;swap(o++,array.indexOf(f[s++])),await sleep()}for(;l<i.length;){if(firstRun)return;swap(o++,array.indexOf(i[l++])),await sleep()}},t=array.length;for(let a=0;a<t;a+=1)await r(a,Math.min(a+1-1,t-1));for(let r=1;r<t;r*=2)for(let e=0;e<t;e+=2*r){const n=e+r-1,f=Math.min(e+2*r-1,t-1);await a(e,n,f)}}
